summary: This source is a brief biographical overview of Hannah Arendt, noting her influence as a 20th-century political theorist. It highlights her focus on totalitarianism, authority, and the trial of Adolf Eichmann, specifically noting her concept of 'the banality of evil' to explain how ordinary people participate in totalitarian systems.

mental_models.name: The banality of evil
mental_models.description: A concept used to explain how ordinary people become actors in totalitarian systems, rather than being inherently monstrous.

signature_quotes.text: Nobody has the right to obey
signature_quotes.context: Featured on a portrait of Hannah Arendt on a wall of her birthplace in Hannover, Germany.
A phrase she coined to explain the actions of ordinary people in totalitarian systems, notably during the trial of Adolf Eichmann.
